type: CLAIM
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py

type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_imageops.py
spec: Please add tests for exif_transpose(image, strip_metadata=True). Behavior to test: exif_transpose gains a new keyword-only parameter strip_metadata (default False). When strip_metadata=True, after performing the normal orientation transpose (or copy, if no orientation tag present / method is None), ALL metadata is stripped from the returned/modified image: image.info should be cleared of exif-related keys (at minimum 'exif', 'Raw profile type exif', 'XML:com.adobe.xmp', 'xmp'), and exif_image.getexif() should return an empty Exif object (len(exif_image.getexif()) == 0). This should work both for in_place=True and in_place=False. Suggested test: open Tests/images/hopper.png or another image with orientation exif, load exif with orientation tag + other tags (e.g. Make/Model or GPS), call ImageOps.exif_transpose(im, strip_metadata=True), then assert the returned image's getexif() is empty and 'exif' not in im.info (or is empty). Also test that when strip_metadata=False (default/omitted), existing non-orientation exif tags are still preserved (existing behavior unchanged). Function signature will be: def exif_transpose(image, *, in_place=False, strip_metadata=False) -> Image.Image | None, with in_place overloads also gaining strip_metadata: bool = False keyword.

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
spec: Add a new keyword-only parameter transform_callback to exif_transpose() (and its two @overload signatures), positioned after in_place. Signature: transform_callback: Callable[[int], Image.Transpose | Sequence[Image.Transpose] | None] | None = None. Behavior: after computing 'orientation = image_exif.get(ExifTags.Base.Orientation, 1)', if transform_callback is not None, call method = transform_callback(orientation) instead of using the hardcoded dict lookup; otherwise keep the existing dict.get(orientation) lookup unchanged (so default behavior is 100% preserved when the callback is omitted). The callback's return value may be: None (treat as a no-op, same as the current 'method is None' case: skip transposition entirely but still proceed to any later metadata-cleanup step you're adding for strip_metadata), a single Image.Transpose value (apply exif_image.transpose(method) exactly as today, either in-place or on a copy per the in_place flag), or a sequence (list/tuple) of Image.Transpose values (apply them in order via successive .transpose(m) calls, in-place or on a copy consistently with the in_place flag). Whichever path is used (dict or callback), the rest of the function (removing the Orientation exif tag, updating exif bytes in image.info, stripping XMP orientation tags) must run unchanged whenever a transposition was actually applied. Add 'from typing import Callable' or 'Sequence' imports as needed at the top of the file if not already present. Please also update the docstring to document transform_callback: 'An optional callable. If provided, it is called with the EXIF orientation value (int) and must return an Image.Transpose value, a sequence of Image.Transpose values to apply in order, or None to skip transposition. Overrides the default orientation-to-method mapping.' No changes needed to Tests/test_imageops.py from you for this parameter — I own that file and will add my own tests for transform_callback there.
note: transform_callback spec for exif_transpose; you own ImageOps.py, please implement this alongside strip_metadata

type: DEFER
files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py
spec: def exif_transpose(image: Image.Image, *, in_place: bool = False, strip_metadata: bool = False) -> Image.Image | None (add strip_metadata: bool = False to both overload signatures too, alongside in_place). Behavior: after the existing transpose/orientation-removal logic runs (i.e. after the existing 'if not in_place: return transposed_image' / 'elif not in_place: return image.copy()' branches would normally return, and after in-place mutation for in_place=True), if strip_metadata is True, strip ALL metadata from exif_image (the transposed/copied/in-place image): (1) call exif_image.info.clear() to remove things like 'exif', 'Raw profile type exif', 'XML:com.adobe.xmp', 'xmp', 'icc_profile' etc, and (2) ensure exif_image.getexif() is empty afterward (e.g. by clearing the Exif dict via exif_image.getexif().clear() before/instead of relying on info.clear(), so len(exif_image.getexif()) == 0). Apply this regardless of whether method was None (i.e. even when there was no orientation tag, if strip_metadata=True the copy/in-place image should still get its metadata stripped). Keep default strip_metadata=False fully backward compatible (no behavior change when omitted). Also add a docstring line: ':param strip_metadata: Boolean. Keyword-only argument. If True, all metadata is removed from the returned image, not just the EXIF orientation tag. Defaults to False.' I already sent a DEFER+SPEC for Tests/test_imageops.py describing the tests I need — please implement those there as well since you're now owning both files.

type: CLAIM
files: Tests/test_imageops.py
note: I'll own the test file and add tests for transform_callback myself. If you need specific test coverage for strip_metadata added there, send me a SPEC describing the test cases and I'll add them; otherwise I'll leave your feature's tests to you to describe.
